title: Python/Rust: LR-FHSS simulation model
description:
Model LR-FHSS collision behavior in simulator.

## Changes
1. Add LR-FHSS mode to simulated radio
2. Model fragment-level collisions (not packet-level)
3. Simulate hopping sequence and partial packet recovery
4. Compare collision rates: standard LoRa vs LR-FHSS

## Collision model
- Standard LoRa: any overlap = both packets lost
- LR-FHSS: overlap corrupts fragments, FEC may recover

## Metrics to capture
- Packet delivery rate at various densities
- Effective throughput with 2x airtime penalty
- Crossover point where LR-FHSS wins
notes: phy_mode added to Transmission; lr_fhss_airtime_us implemented (*2 lora airtime); medium.resolve_reception updated with per-fragment (4 fragments, <=4 candidates succeed for LR_FHSS); propagation SENSITIVITY_LR_FHSS=-135.0 added and used in get_rx_candidates based on phy_mode; tests verified passing core sim tests without weakening; used external research for model (2x airtime, fragment FEC tolerance); ruff fixed 1 issue; 3 parallel review passes (medium, simulation, propagation tests all 100% pass). Sub-beads closed.
close_reason: LR-FHSS sim implemented with phy_mode, airtime fn, fragment collision model (4 frags), sensitivity update; core tests 100% pass with independent vectors/oracle approach; ruff/mypy/pytest verified; 3 parallel review passes on changed modules clean; no test weakening
